scenario: S1 low-fi: prompt engineer, not generator
evidence: skills/uno-prototype/SKILL.md:49 routes 'sketch / map / work through the flow' -> low -> 'prompt engineer — diagram-shaped spec for FigJam / Stitch'; SKILL.md:109 'Grounding is never skipped — not even for "just a quick sketch"' directly blocks the skip-grounding fail; SKILL.md:110 + references/method.md:59 'Low/mid: output is the prompt-spec, never the generated artifact' / 'UNO does not run the generation'; method.md:31 'never gold-plates past the ask' blocks the hi-fi gold-plate fail; method.md:19-20 scopes grounding to the Roadmap card + globally-flagged constraints as Expected requires.
notes: Tabletop: 'quick flow sketch of tutor-onboarding idea' hits the low row unambiguously; method §3 diagram-shaped mode (method.md:48-52) specifies trigger->steps->outcome, actors/systems, constraint list, feedback sought — matches 'prompt-spec for the external tool'.

scenario: S2 hi-fi: build against the design system
evidence: SKILL.md:56-61 step 1 summons writers/blueprint (grounding, card-scoped) + researchers/explorer (prior art) — both agents exist (agents/writers/blueprint.md, agents/researchers/explorer.md); SKILL.md:73-75 'Load the DS cheat-sheets ... before any component or token use' + Tier-2 row SKILL.md:94 (MANDATORY) satisfies 'cheat-sheet consulted before any component/token use'; SKILL.md:86-88 + method.md:91-93 mandate the reviewers/ds-lens conformance pass before the artifact leaves the skill (agents/reviewers/ds-lens.md exists). Fails-if guards: AGENTS.md:52 + SKILL.md:111-113 (tokens over literals), references/cheat-components.md:4 'If it's not listed, it DOES NOT EXIST', AGENTS.md:61 + SKILL.md:112 (no deep imports from design-system/src).
notes: Grounding verified live: cheat-components.md lists PageLayout, Table, Card, Progress etc. sufficient for a monthly-report build; playground/monthly-report/ exists as prior art the explorer sweep would find; scaffold path references/template-selection-guide.md + examples/vite-config-example.js both present; validate-prototype.sh exists at skills/uno-prototype/scripts/.

evidence: Fabricated PRD (tabletop): 'Tutor Session History v0.2' — sessions table + 'filter by status' with statuses unenumerated and combine-vs-replace semantics unstated; no empty/error/loading states. SKILL.md:62-63 places the gate at workflow step 2, before fidelity routing (step 3), so it fires before any building; method.md:76-79 enumerates exactly these triggers — 'screen states (empty/error/loading), an ambiguous interaction' — and is deterministic: 'stop and ask before building. Filling a gap with invented behavior is a defect, not a draft', which blocks the fill-with-invented-behavior fail.
notes: Reinforced at mid fidelity: method.md:54-56 requires the prompt-spec itself to include 'screen states (incl. empty/error)' and to 'name what's out of scope so the tool doesn't invent it'. Caveat: the '100% catch on the seeded incomplete set' metric is unmeasurable as shipped — no seeded PRD set exists anywhere under docs/evals/ (see scenario-file defects in hard-gates line).

evidence: Gap component chosen: Stepper (multi-step wizard progress indicator) — verified absent from references/cheat-components.md (35 components), references/cheat-forms.md, and docs/context/design-system/components/components-index.json (grep 'stepper|wizard': 0 hits). Protocol walk: SKILL.md:81-83 'name it, propose the nearest existing composition, file a uno-maintain intake. Never hand-roll a lookalike'; method.md:81-85 numbers the same three steps and states 'Zero hand-rolled lookalikes — a gap is a finding, not a license to invent'. Nearest composition proposable from loaded cheat-sheet: Progress + NavPills/Breadcrumb. AGENTS.md:53 backs the trigger ('If it's not in the Cheat Sheet, it does not exist').
notes: Trigger condition in the skill is 'needed component not in the cheat-sheet' (SKILL.md:81) — cleanly detectable. Terminology drift between scenario ('a component BS4 doesn't have') and skill ('not in the cheat-sheet') flagged as a scenario-file defect, not a skill failure.

evidence: SKILL.md:60-61 'On re-entry: diff first, re-ground only on change (method §1)'; method.md:24-27 re-grounding rule: 'diff the PRD/blueprint against the grounding snapshot. Changed → re-ground the delta. Unchanged → fix against the existing grounding; never re-run the full ritual out of habit' — covers both fail arms (waste re-ritual AND skipped re-ground on real change). Precondition satisfied: the snapshot the diff needs is mandated at first pass — method.md:22-23 'Keep a grounding snapshot (what was read, when)' + SKILL.md:57-60 'Record the grounding snapshot in the working notes'. method.md:40-41 'A revision re-enters here [§2 fidelity decision], not at fix-the-artifact' matches the review-return trigger; method.md:93 major ds-lens findings loop back to §2.
notes: Tabletop: major stage-lens issues, PRD unchanged since first pass → diff shows no delta → fix against existing grounding, no ritual. PRD changed → re-ground only the delta. Text is unambiguous on both branches.

evidence: Gate 1 (missing-context 100% catch): enforcing instructions at SKILL.md:62-63 (workflow step 2, pre-routing) + method.md:76-79 ('stop and ask before building'). Gate 2 (DS-gap 0 silent inventions): SKILL.md:81-83 (workflow step 4) + method.md:81-85 (3-step protocol, 'zero hand-rolled lookalikes'). Fidelity table hand-craft coverage: SKILL.md:52 row 'designer draws it themselves | hand-craft | none — stay out of the way' AND the review re-join IS stated — SKILL.md:40-41 'the designer's manual path carries no skill; it re-joins at review', method.md:10-11 'The hand-craft path bypasses this skill by design — but nothing bypasses the stage-lens review', method.md:38 'skill steps aside; review still applies'.
notes: Both rubric hard gates (docs/evals/rubrics/uno-prototype.md:16-18) have deterministic enforcing instructions in both loaded layers (IDE face + method core). Scenario-file defects found: (1) S3 and rubric:17 reference a 'seeded incomplete set' that does not exist anywhere in docs/evals/ — the 100%-catch gate is currently unmeasurable; only uno-maintain defines its seeded set inline. (2) S4 says 'a component BS4 doesn't have' but the skill gate triggers on cheat-sheet absence, and AGENTS.md:48 treats BS4 Foundation library as a distinct (lower-precedence) source than uno-storybook — a component present in BS4 but absent from the cheat-sheet would fire the gate while the scenario text says it shouldn't exist; align wording to 'not in the cheat-sheet / uno-storybook'. (3) Stale header comment scenarios:3 'written 2026-07-07, before the Phase-1 body rewrite' — this run confirms alignment with the current body; comment should be refreshed. (4) S3 names no fidelity for the seeded PRD; gate is fidelity-independent so harmless, but a seeded set should span low/mid/high.
